raw_consumer_statement:
Here 's the CFPB " What happened? '' text for Barclays copy and paste : On XX/XX/year>, a charge of {$6400.00} was posted to my Barclays credit card for credit funding consulting and house renovation services provided by XXXX XXXX XXXX, processed through their merchant processor FanBasis. No services were ever delivered. 
The CEO of XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, sent a written email on XX/XX/year> confirming that no services would be rendered, that I am fully entitled to a refund, and that he would not appeal any chargeback. In a second email dated XX/XX/year>, he confirmed that XXXX  falsely notified banks that services were delivered without his authorization, which he stated may constitute a violation of the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( EFTA ) and unfair and deceptive practices under the FTC Act.

I filed a chargeback with Barclays for {$6400.00}. The dispute was denied despite having written confirmation from the merchant that no services were rendered. XXXX made false representations to Barclays claiming services were delivered, causing my dispute to be wrongfully rejected. 
I am requesting the CFPB investigate this matter and assist in recovering the {$6400.00} that was wrongfully charged and remains unrefunded.
